rationale: ll-* SKILL.md are uniformly thin Codex bridges to pre-existing targets; bare-named skills hold content. CompactionConfig.enabled gates opt-in LLM cost not structural safety, so gating eviction on it defeats its instant-fallback purpose (100% of installs default it off).
rule: Skill: full content in skills/compact-session/SKILL.md; Eviction: always-on, independent of CompactionConfig.enabled
alternatives_rejected: Stub SKILL.md + new commands/compact-session.md; eviction gated on CompactionConfig.enabled
